_README: Copy this file to config/system.json and fill in the values for YOUR setup. Pull the RF values (control channel, site frequency spread, system type Phase I vs II) from your RadioReference export for Orange County CCCS. Anything in ALL_CAPS or marked CHANGE_ME must be edited.

icecast._serverReachableBase: The base URL the SafeT SERVER uses to pull each stream. The bridge worker (ffmpeg) runs on the SafeT server, NOT on this PC. If you self-host SafeT on THIS same PC, leave it as 127.0.0.1. If SafeT runs in the cloud (Railway), this PC's localhost is NOT reachable from there — expose Icecast with a tunnel (see README 'Cloud SafeT') and put the public URL here, e.g. https://occcs.example.trycloudflare.com

sdr._comment: One Nooelec v5 (RTL-SDR, R820T2). Stay at or below 2.56 MHz: 2.88+ Msps drops samples over USB/usbipd/WSL and the P25 decoder gets pure noise (decode rate 0/sec on every control channel — observed in the field at 3.2M). 'gain' 0 = auto AGC; set a fixed dB (e.g. 36-49) once you tune. 'ppm' from your dongle's frequency error. The OC CCCS Countywide site spans 855.7125-860.9625 MHz (5.25 MHz) — wider than ANY single dongle. Field logs show the site parks mostly on the HIGH control-channel pair, so center 859.8 @ 2.544 MHz (rates MUST be multiples of 24000 — the P25 symbol rate — or trunk-recorder aborts on boot; 2.4M=100x and 2.544M=106x are valid, 2.56M and 3.2M are NOT) covers both (860.2125c, 860.4625c) plus the upper nine voice frequencies; it is deaf only while the site rests on a low CC.

system._comment: From RadioReference. 'type' is p25 — trunk-recorder auto-detects Phase I vs Phase II (TDMA) per call, so there is NO phase flag to set. 'modulation' is the CONTROL-CHANNEL modulation and is the knob that matters: 'qpsk' for CQPSK/LSM simulcast (most P25 Phase II county systems), 'fsk4' for C4FM. List EVERY control-channel-capable frequency the site has (RadioReference marks them 'c') — the site ROTATES its control channel among them, and trunk-recorder can only follow to ones inside a dongle's window; any it can't reach is a dead period. OC CCCS Countywide (site 021): 856.7125c, 857.4625c, 860.2125c, 860.4625c.

_multiDongle: RECOMMENDED for OC CCCS — two dongles cover all FOUR control channels (no dead periods) and 20 of the site's 22 frequencies at the safe 2.56 Msps rate. Replace the single 'sdr' object with this 'sources' array. The talkgroups you pick in the SafeT console always attach to the FIRST system.

_systems_example._note: a second P25 system on UHF, decoded by the 2nd dongle

safet._comment: An ADMIN account on your SafeT-PTT server. baseUrl ends in /v1. agencySlug only needed for non-owner multi-tenant logins (leave "" otherwise).

bridgeDefaults._comment: Applied to every bridge unless overridden per-entry below. voxThreshold 0-1 (lower = more sensitive). yieldToUnits=false keeps scanner traffic on-air even when a real unit keys up; set true if you want live units to pre-empt the feed.

_bridges_comment: OPTIONAL — only used by the offline `npm run generate` path (B). If you pick talkgroups in the SafeT console (Bridges → Import from RadioReference) and run `npm start`, this list is IGNORED. For the offline path: 'tgid' is the decimal Talkgroup ID, 'channel' is the SafeT channel name, 'mount' should be tg<TGID> (e.g. tg16) to match the console convention.
